name: bibt-gcp-scc

metadata.name: bibt-gcp-scc
metadata.summary: Functionality often used by BITS Blue Team (GCP SCC Module).

documentation:
# bibt-gcp-scc: BITS Blue Team Utilities: GCP Security Command Center Module

- **Developer**: Matthew OBrien
- **Email**: mobrien@broadinstitute.org
- **Project URL**: https://pypi.org/project/bibt-gcp-scc/
- **Project Repo**: https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-scc
- **Project Documentation**: https://bibt-gcp-scc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

## Installing

```bash
$ pip install --upgrade bibt-gcp-scc
```

## Testing

- Requires Docker to be installed on your system.
- Clone this repo and create & run the test container:

```
$ docker build -f ./Dockerfile -t bibt-gcp-scc-test . && docker run bibt-gcp-scc-test
```

## Usage

- See documentation here: https://bibt-gcp-scc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

## All BIBT Libraries

- [bibt-gcp-asset](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-asset)
- [bibt-gcp-bq](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-bq)
- [bibt-gcp-iam](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-iam)
- [bibt-gcp-pubsub](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-pubsub)
- [bibt-gcp-scc](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-scc)
- [bibt-gcp-secrets](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-secrets)
- [bibt-gcp-storage](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-gcp-storage)
- [bibt-qualys](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-qualys)
- [bibt-sentinelone](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-sentinelone)
- [bibt-slack](https://github.com/broadinstitute/bibt-slack)

classes.name: bibt.gcp.scc.classes.FindingInfo

methods.name: bibt.gcp.scc.classes.FindingInfo.package
documentation: Converts this object into a dict.

This class compiles information related to a given SCC finding in a standard
way. One of the issues with SCC findings is that different SCC sources pass
different fields; here, we can standardize how fields are passed around in
functions and pipelines.

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods.get_all_findings
documentation:
Returns an iterator for all findings matching a particular filter.

```python
from bibt.gcp.scc import get_all_findings
for _ in get_all_findings(
    filter='category="PUBLIC_BUCKET_ACL"',
    order_by='eventTime desc',
    gcp_org_id=123123
):
    print(_.finding.name, _.resource.name)
```

```
type filter



param filter

the filter to use. See
here
for more on valid filter syntax.

type gcp_org_id



param gcp_org_id

the GCP organization ID under which to search.

type order_by



param order_by

(optional) the sort order of the findings. See
here
for more on valid arguments. Default is None.

type page_size



param page_size

(optional) the page size for the API requests.
max and default is 1000 .

type credentials



param credentials

(optional) the credentials object to use when making the
API call, if not to use the account running the function for authentication.

type client



param client

(optional) the SCC client to use for API calls.
will generate one if not passed.

rtype



returns

an iterator for all findings matching the filter.
```

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods.get_value
documentation:
Fetches the value in the given `obj` according to the given `path`.

: Works on objects and dicts. Supports arrays in a few ways:

- if the `path` is `resource.folders[].resource_folder_display_name` OR
  `resource.folders[0].resource_folder_display_name`,
  it will just consider the first element in the array.
- if the `path` is `resource.folders[*].resource_folder_display_name`,
  it will return a list of `resource_folder_display_name` values,
  one for each folder.

Additionally, if unsuccessful with exactly what was passed as `path`, it
will convert and try both camelized and underscored attribute names
(`resource_folder_display_name` and `resourceFolderDisplayName`).
As a last resort it will try a key lookup (e.g. `obj[key]`).

```python
from bibt.gcp import scc
f = scc.get_finding(
    name="organizations/123123/sources/123123/findings/123123",
    gcp_org_id=123123
)
v = scc.get_value(
    f,
    "finding.source_properties.abuse_target_ips"
)
print(v)
```

```
type obj



param obj

the object from which to extract a value.

type path



param path

the path to follow to find the desired value(s).

type raise_exception



param raise_exception

whether it should raise an exception if the path isn't
resolved successfully, or just return None.

returns

whatever it finds at the end of the specified path.

raises KeyError

if the next part of the path cannot be found.
```

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods.get_finding
documentation:
This function returns the finding object specified by name.

```python
from bibt.gcp import scc
f = scc.get_finding(
    name="organizations/123123/sources/123123/findings/123123",
    gcp_org_id=123123
)
print(f.finding.name, f.resource.name)
```

```
type name



param name

the finding.name to fetch.

type gcp_org_id



param gcp_org_id

the GCP organization ID under which to search.

type credentials



param credentials

the credentials object to use when making the API call, if not to
use the account running the function for authentication.

type client



param client

(optional) the SCC client to use for API calls.
will generate one if not passed.

rtype



returns

the specified finding object, paired with its resource information.

raises ValueError

if no finding under the supplied name is found.
```

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods.get_security_marks
documentation:
Gets security marks on an asset or finding in SCC and returns them as a dict.

```python
from bibt.gcp import scc
for k, v in scc.get_security_marks(
    scc_name="organizations/123123/sources/123123/findings/123123",
    os.environ["GCP_ORG_ID"]
).items():
    print(k, v)
```

```
type scc_name



param scc_name

may be either an SCC finding.name or a GCP resourceName .
format is: organizations/123123/sources/123123/findings/123123 or
//storage.googleapis.com/my-bucket.
note this does not accept ``asset.name`` format!

type gcp_org_id



param gcp_org_id

the GCP organization ID under which to search.

type credentials



param credentials

the credentials object to use when making the API call, if
not to use the account running the function for authentication.

type client



param client

(optional) the SCC client to use for API calls.
will generate one if not passed.

rtype



returns

a dictionary containing security marks as key/value pairs.

raises TypeError

if scc_name is not in a recognizeable format.
```

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods.get_sources
documentation:
Returns a list of all sources in the parent.

```python
for source in get_sources("organizations/123456"):
    print(source.display_name)
```

```
type parent_name



param parent_name

the parent name, e.g. "organizations/123456" or
"projects/123456"

type credentials



param credentials

the credentials object to use when making the API call,
if not to use the account running the function for authentication.

type client



param client

(optional) the SCC client to use for API calls.
will generate one if not passed.

rtype

 

returns

a list of SCC Source objects
```

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods.parse_notification
documentation:
This method takes the notification received from a SCC Notification Pubsub
and returns a Python object.

```python
import base64
from bibt.gcp import scc
def main(event, context):
    raw_notification = base64.b64decode(event["data"]).decode("utf-8")
    notification = scc.parse_notification(raw_notification)
    print(
        notification.finding.name,
        notification.finding.category,
        notification.resource.name
    )
```

```
type notification

 OR 

param notification

the notification to parse. may be either a dictionary
or a json string.

type ignore_unknown_fields



param ignore_unknown_fields

whether or not unrecognized fields should be
ignored when parsing. fields may be unrecognized if they are added to
the finding category in later releases of google-cloud-securitycenter library.

rtype



returns

the finding notification as a Python object.

raises TypeError

if it is passed anything aside from a 
or , or it has an issue parsing the finding into an object.
```

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods.set_finding_state
documentation:
This method will set the finding to inactive state by default.

```python
from bibt.gcp import scc
scc.set_finding_state(
    finding_name="organizations/123123/sources/123123/findings/123123"
)
```

```
type finding_name



param finding_name

the finding.name whose state to modify.

type state



param state

the state to set the finding to. must be valid according to
.
defaults to "INACTIVE".

type credentials



param credentials

the credentials object to use when making the API call,
if not to use the account running the function for authentication.

type client



param client

(optional) the SCC client to use for API calls.
will generate one if not passed.

raises KeyError

if the argument supplied for state is not a valid name
for .
```

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods.set_security_marks
documentation:
Sets security marks on an asset or finding in SCC. Usually, if we're setting

: them on a finding, it means we're setting a mark of `reason` for setting it
  to inactive. if we're setting them on an asset, it is usually to
  `allow_{finding.category}=true` .

```python
from bibt.gcp import scc
scc.set_security_mark(
    scc_name="organizations/123123/sources/123123/findings/123123",
    marks={
        'reason': 'intentionally public'
    }
)
```

```
type scc_name



param scc_name

may be either an SCC finding.name or a GCP resourceName .
format is: organizations/123123/sources/123123/findings/123123 or
//storage.googleapis.com/my-bucket. note this does not accept
``asset.name`` format!

type marks



param marks

a dictionary of marks to set on the asset or finding. format it:
marks={"allow_public_bucket_acl": "true", "reason": "intentional"} .
note this must be a dict and not a list!

type credentials



param credentials

the credentials object to use when making the API call, if not
to use the account running the function for authentication.

type client



param client

(optional) the SCC client to use for API calls. will generate one
if not passed.

raises TypeError

if the argument supplied for marks is not a 
```

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods.set_mute_status
documentation:
This method will mute the finding by default. May also be used to unmute with
`status="UNMUTED"` .

```python
from bibt.gcp import scc
scc.set_mute_status(
    finding_name="organizations/123123/sources/123123/findings/123123"
)
```

```
type finding_name



param finding_name

the finding.name whose state to modify.

type status



param status

whether the finding should be muted or unmuted. must be a valid
value of MUTED or UNMUTED . defaults to MUTED .

type credentials



param credentials

the credentials object to use when making the API call, if
not to use the account running the function for authentication.

type client



param client

(optional) the SCC client to use for API calls. will generate one
if not passed.

raises KeyError

if the argument supplied for status is not MUTED
or UNMUTED .
```

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods._get_all_findings_iter
documentation:
A helper method to make a list_findings API call. Expects a valid `request`
dictionary and can optionally be supplied with a credentials object.

Returns: {py:class}`gcp_scc:google.cloud.securitycenter_v1.services.security_center.pagers.ListFindingsPager`

functions.name: bibt.gcp.scc.methods._get
documentation:
A helper function to get attributes. Works with objects as well as dictionaries.
    Will attempt in this order: 1) exactly what was passed (obj.my_attr) 2)
    underscored (obj.my_attr) 3) camelized (obj.myAttr) 4) key (obj[attr])

Returns: whatever the value of the attribute is.
Raises: KeyError if the key could not be found in the object.
